era: 1991-1995. Jazz Rap. E=5 -- Grammy energy, genre fusion, Ishmael Butler arc. Pendulum Records / Elektra. FIRST JAZZ RAP in the engine -- Genre Stage 99. Central District roots (Butler). Grammy 1994. Ishmael Butler later formed Shabazz Palaces (degree 103). 41-degree arc (103-144) matching Sabzi's span.

location.formative: Ishmael Butler grew up in Seattle's Central District. Moved to New York for Howard University. Formed Digable Planets in NYC. Jazz-hip-hop fusion in early 1990s NYC/DC scene.
location.career: Digable Planets. Jazz Rap trio: Butterfly (Ishmael Butler), Doodlebug (Craig Irving), Ladybug Mecca (Mary Ann Vieira). Two albums: Reachin' (1993), Blowout Comb (1994). Grammy 1994. Butler returned to Seattle, formed Shabazz Palaces.

members.name: Ishmael Butler (Butterfly)
members.note: Central District roots. Abstract poetry. Later formed Shabazz Palaces (degree 103). 41-degree arc (103-144). The Seattle connection.

members.name: Craig Irving (Doodlebug)
members.note: Philadelphia. Political consciousness. East Coast lyrical tradition.

associated_musicians.name: Shabazz Palaces
associated_musicians.note: Ishmael Butler + Tendai Maraire. Experimental hip-hop. Sub Pop. Afrofuturist. 41-degree arc from 103 to 144.

associated_musicians.name: A Tribe Called Quest
associated_musicians.note: Parallel Jazz Rap practitioners. The Low End Theory. Genre comparison.

associated_musicians.name: Sabzi
associated_musicians.note: Sabzi's 41-degree span matches Butler's 41-degree span. Engine echo.

theory_nodes.concept: First Jazz Rap -- Genre Stage 99; jazz + hip-hop fusion; jazz samples, live instrumentation, jazz sensibility, bohemian identity; the first genre that fuses two distinct traditions
examples: Jazz samples: Art Blakey, Herbie Hancock, Curtis Fuller.
Live instrumentation: upright bass, saxophone, piano, drums.
Bohemian identity: coffee houses, poetry, jazz clubs.
'Rebirth of Slick (Cool Like Dat)' -- the defining Jazz Rap hit.
theory_nodes.description: First Jazz Rap. Genre fusion. Jazz meets hip-hop.

ancestors: A Tribe Called Quest, De La Soul.
genealogy.relationship: Digable Planets at 144. First Jazz Rap in engine.

theory_nodes.concept: Grammy achievement -- 1994 Best Rap Performance by Duo/Group; highest commercial recognition in hip-hop era; proved Jazz Rap commercially viable
examples: Grammy 1994: Best Rap Performance by a Duo or Group.
'Rebirth of Slick': #15 Billboard Hot 100, #1 Hot Rap Singles.
Proved Jazz Rap could be commercially successful.
theory_nodes.description: Grammy 1994. Commercial peak. Jazz Rap viable.

theory_nodes.concept: Ishmael Butler arc -- Digable Planets (144) to Shabazz Palaces (103); 41-degree span; from jazz-cool to Afrofuturist experiment; 41-degree echo with Sabzi (99-140)
examples: 103: Shabazz Palaces (experimental, Sub Pop, dark, abstract).
144: Digable Planets (Jazz Rap, Grammy, cool, accessible).
Two figures, two arcs, same span. Engine echo.
theory_nodes.description: Butler arc. 41 degrees. Cool to experimental.

genealogy.relationship: Digable Planets at 144. Butler arc endpoint.

cross_references.relationship: BUTLER ARC! Ishmael Butler. 41-degree span (103-144). Shabazz Palaces (experimental, dark, Sub Pop) to Digable Planets (Jazz Rap, cool, Grammy). Same artist, opposite aesthetics.
41-DEGREE ECHO! Sabzi's span (99-140 = 41 degrees) matches Butler's span (103-144 = 41 degrees). Two Seattle figures, two arcs, same distance.
PREVIOUS DEGREE! The hip-hop era shifts from geography (Tacoma at 143) to genre (Jazz Rap at 144).
ERA SPAN! Eleven degrees (134-144). DJs > producers > collectives > organizations > cities > genres. The most comprehensive era documentation.
